sample_generated.stimulus: If a bill has majority support, then it becomes law. This bill became law. Therefore, this bill had majority support.
sample_generated.question: The reasoning above is most vulnerable to criticism on the grounds that it
choices: draws a general conclusion from a sample that is too small or unrepresentative to support it
assumes that what is true of each part must be true of the whole
presupposes the truth of the very conclusion it is trying to establish
concludes that a sufficient condition was met merely because the result it guarantees occurred
treats two options as the only possibilities when other alternatives are available
sample_generated.explanation: This argument commits affirming the consequent: it concludes that a sufficient condition was met merely because the result it guarantees occurred. (Source: Affirming the consequent (Wikipedia))
